title: Beilschmiedia glaucoides
taxonomy: Beilschmiedia glaucoides is classified within the family Lauraceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as laurels. This family is part of the order Laurales, a group of flowering plants that are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. The Lauraceae family is characterized by flowers with a three-parted calyx and a five-parted corolla. The Beilschmiedia genus is native to tropical Africa and Madagascar, with Beilschmiedia glaucoides being a specific species within this genus. The classification of Beilschmiedia glaucoides helps to understand its evolutionary relationships and characteristics within the larger plant kingdom.
